title: DeepSwapAI face swap evaluation metric decision map
alternateName: Face swap benchmark metric selection matrix
description: A source-cited decision map for selecting face swap identity, target-attribute, distribution, temporal, no-reference, or human-review evidence without combining incompatible protocols.
creator: DeepSwapAI Product Team
attribution: DeepSwapAI Face Swap Evaluation Metric Decision Map v1, https://deepswapai.com/face-swap-quality-scorecard#automated-benchmark-map
scope: A literature-grounded selection map describing what seven evaluation families compare, the protocol level they require, and the limits on interpreting their values.
evidenceBoundary: This map does not run any metric, score any face swap output, or compare any provider.
A metric name does not make values portable across datasets, evaluators, crops, preprocessing, implementations, sample counts, or aggregation rules.
Identity, target-attribute preservation, distribution realism, temporal behavior, no-reference quality, and human review answer different questions.
Human review, consent, disclosure, and use-specific acceptance remain separate from an automated benchmark value.
protocolChecklist: Name the source, target, output, reference set, or frame sequence compared by each measure.
Pin the dataset version, evaluator implementation and weights, crop, alignment, color pipeline, and preprocessing.
Report sample count, repeated runs, failed samples, aggregation, dispersion, and confidence intervals where applicable.
Keep identity transfer, target preservation, set-level realism, temporal behavior, and human acceptance as separately inspectable results.

metrics.name: Identity retrieval or embedding similarity
metrics.compares: Source identity versus the swapped output, using a named face encoder or source gallery.
metrics.question: How strongly does the output retain source-identity evidence under the fixed evaluator?
metrics.direction: Higher similarity or retrieval performance is usually better inside the same protocol.
prerequisites: Documented detection, alignment, crop, and aggregation
metrics.boundary: The encoder, crop, gallery, demographics, threshold, and preprocessing affect the value. It is not a biometric verdict for one person.

metrics.name: Pose and expression error
metrics.compares: Target pose or expression estimates versus the swapped output under a named estimator.
metrics.question: How closely does the output preserve target head pose and expression under the fixed estimator?
metrics.direction: Lower error is usually better inside the same estimator and parameterization.
prerequisites: Documented parameter space, crop, and aggregation
metrics.boundary: Values are not portable across different estimators, crops, parameter spaces, landmark conventions, or preprocessing pipelines.

metrics.name: Frechet Inception Distance (FID)
metrics.compares: A distribution of generated images or frames versus a documented reference distribution.
metrics.question: How close are two feature distributions under the fixed feature extractor and sampling protocol?
metrics.direction: Lower is better only inside the same dataset, extractor, preprocessing, and sample protocol.
prerequisites: Documented sample count, resize, color, and aggregation
metrics.boundary: One face swap output cannot have a defensible FID because a single image is not a distribution. FID does not isolate identity correctness.

metrics.name: Subject and background consistency
metrics.compares: Feature consistency across output frames and against preceding, target, or reference frames.
metrics.question: How stable are subject and background features through motion under the fixed video protocol?
metrics.direction: Higher consistency is usually better under the same implementation.
prerequisites: Documented reference relationship and temporal aggregation
metrics.boundary: Consistency alone cannot establish correct identity mapping, visual realism, motion accuracy, or publication readiness.

metrics.name: Gaze, eye aperture, lip sync, and motion diagnostics
metrics.compares: Named eye, audio-lip, landmark, or optical-flow behavior between target and output sequences.
metrics.question: Which specific dynamic target attributes remain synchronized through the generated video?
metrics.direction: Direction and unit depend on the named diagnostic; report each separately.
prerequisites: Documented units, missing-frame policy, and aggregation
metrics.boundary: These diagnostics are not interchangeable, need a documented video protocol, and do not apply to every media type.

metrics.name: Learned no-reference quality assessment
metrics.compares: The output alone, evaluated by a model trained against ranked human judgments or labeled quality data.
metrics.question: How does a trained assessor rank visible output quality when explicit reference media are unavailable?
metrics.direction: Direction depends on the model output; validate it on a documented held-out distribution.
metrics.boundary: Performance depends on training data and validation scope. An output-only score cannot establish correct source identity transfer by itself.

metrics.name: Structured human scorecard
metrics.compares: One visible output against explicit defect anchors, publication gates, and reviewer notes.
metrics.question: Which visible defects or unresolved use gates does a reviewer observe in this specific output?
metrics.direction: Use anchored ordinal ratings; do not reinterpret the result as a probability or biometric score.
prerequisites: Applicable criteria, reviewer notes, and unresolved gates
metrics.boundary: Subjective evidence about the reviewed sample, not model accuracy, provider ranking, probability, or biometric similarity.

references.name: Towards High Fidelity Face Swapping: A Comprehensive Survey and New Benchmark
references.relationship: Defines the CASIA FaceSwapping benchmark and documents identity, target-attribute, distribution, and video consistency protocols.

references.name: CanonSwap: High-Fidelity and Consistent Video Face Swapping via Canonical Space Modulation
references.relationship: Reports video-specific identity, target-attribute, synchronization, temporal consistency, and distribution measures.

references.name: Rank-based No-reference Quality Assessment for Face Swapping
references.relationship: Studies learned output-only face-swap quality assessment trained against ranked judgments.

references.name: GANs Trained by a Two Time-Scale Update Rule Converge to a Local Nash Equilibrium
references.relationship: Introduces Frechet Inception Distance as a distribution-level evaluation measure.

references.name: ITU-R BT.500-15
references.relationship: Primary standard for documented subjective image assessment methodology.

references.name: ITU-T P.910 (10/2023)
references.relationship: Primary standard for documented subjective multimedia assessment methodology.
